As a renter, There should be one uniform rental agreement for housing. Each state has its rules, so some addtional info must be included. But the vast majority of info in a lease is universal. My position is in favor of the proposed law. I believe that there are no real drawbacks with serious consequences, and the only people who this doesn’t help are landlords preying on naive renters who fall victim to deceptive tactics. Rental contracts should have clear, easy to read disclosures at the top of any rental agreement, specifying both the base rent along with a clear listing of additional (mandatory and optional) fees the renter may be subject to. An additional rule is unnecessary: Here in New York, the rental agreement (lease) discloses all fees and obligations of the Tenant. I’m very much in favor of banning hidden rental fees. These deceptive fees harm tenants by setting them up to take on more rent than they can afford. In Utah we have an affordable housing shortage, so a rule to protect renters that spans the entire lease period, from applying for a unit to moving out, is especially important. PLEASE please do something about these hidden mandatory fees. It is simply common sense that one should be able to know the total cost before committing to paying, renting etc. Such underhanded and dishonest practices give capitalism and the free market a bad name and diminish freedom.

I would support requiring landlords to show renters why rental prices are rising and how much this changes their percent profit. I would support protecting renters whose landlords don't comply from having to pay the higher rentals. I would also want them to give a breakdown of any supply chain issues that contribute to rate hikes.
